The article reports kinetic parameters only for the oxidase‑like activity of CuMnO₂ nanoflowers (CuMnO₂NFs) toward the oxidation of TMB. Table 1 gives the Michaelis constant (Kₘ = 0.0499 mM) and the maximal rate (Vₘₐₓ = 75.04 × 10⁻⁸ M s⁻¹) for this reaction. The kinetic assay was performed by varying the TMB concentration from 0 to 0.8 mM while keeping the catalyst amount constant (5 µg in 0.2 mL, i.e. 0.025 mg mL⁻¹). The optimal experimental conditions were pH 4.0 and 30 °C. The nanoflowers have an average particle size of ~400 nm (SEM/TEM). No crystal system (syngony) or surface modification is reported, so those fields are left null. The reaction type is simply “TMB” because no H₂O₂ co‑substrate is involved in the oxidase‑like process.

All required fields (formula, activity) are filled, and the extracted values follow the units specified in the schema.
